methods.description: The stepwise regression method uses a bidirectional elimination approach to model the drift and diffusion functions by adding or removing predictors based on AIC improvement.

steps.description: Start with no predictors for the drift function.
At each step until convergence, add or remove an orthonormal polynomial term by comparing the AIC improvement.
steps.searchable_summary: add or remove polynomial terms based on AIC

steps.description: Stop when no more predictors can be added or removed from the model.
steps.searchable_summary: finalize model when no further AIC improvement

steps.description: Repeat the method for the diffusion term.
steps.searchable_summary: apply stepwise regression to diffusion term

methods.description: The SGP method is applied to capture the physical states of time series and estimate a state-dependent diffusion model.

steps.description: Select the same kernel for estimating both the drift and diffusion terms.
steps.searchable_summary: choose kernel for drift and diffusion estimation

steps.description: Estimate the mean transition time between DO events using the SGP method.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate mean transition time with SGP

steps.description: Provide evidence supporting a novel state-dependent diffusion model for DO events.

methods.description: Validation of the SGP method by comparing its performance with other methods using simulated SDEs.

steps.description: Compare SGP method performance with kernel-based and orthonormal polynomials methods using simulated SDEs.
steps.searchable_summary: compare SGP with KBR and POLY on simulated SDEs

steps.description: Use tests to investigate the impact of the number of pseudo-inputs on the estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: test impact of pseudo-inputs on estimates

steps.description: Generate 100 time series with a length of 104 samples for each model.

methods.description: Application of the SGP method to real-world data, including financial and paleoclimatology data, to illustrate its applicability.

steps.description: Construct daily logarithmic increments of the oil price to obtain a stationary time series.
Apply the SGP method using m = 10 inducing points and two squared exponential kernels.
steps.searchable_summary: apply SGP method with specified parameters

steps.description: Select final estimates using the lower bound.
steps.searchable_summary: select final estimates based on lower bound
